description_long: This is a vintage gramophone-style record player with a large, flared metal horn finished in a reddish-brown patina, giving it an antique appearance. The horn is supported by a curved metal bracket and is attached to a rectangular wooden base with rounded corners. The base features a hinged lid, which is open to reveal a black turntable platter with a visible record on it. There are mechanical components, including a winding handle and exposed gears, on the side of the base, suggesting manual operation. The surface of the wood is worn, with visible grain and some metallic accents, contributing to its aged look. The overall design is ornate and decorative, typical of early 20th-century phonographs.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large reddish-brown metal horn, wooden base, and exposed mechanical parts. The open lid reveals a black turntable platter and a visible record.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full curve of the large metal horn, the open wooden lid, and the winding handle on the side.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide opening of the horn, the record on the platter, and the symmetrical design of the base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the horn's curvature, the open lid, and the mechanical components on the side.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the horn, the open lid, and the exposed hinge mechanism.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present and visible beneath the open lid, appearing as a flat, circular component with a black finish. It is medium-sized relative to the base, with a vinyl record placed on top. The platter surface is smooth, and the record has a central label, but no additional grooves or patterns are visible on the platter itself.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, extending from the side of the base toward the platter. It is slender, metallic, and appears to be positioned over the record, with a visible joint near its base. The finish is metallic, matching the mechanical style of the device.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is not clearly visible, but its presence is implied at the end of the tonearm where it would contact the record.
attribute_control_knobs: A winding handle is visible on the side of the base, serving as a control mechanism. No other control knobs, switches, or buttons are visible.
attribute_dust_cover: The record player has a hinged wooden lid that acts as a dust cover. It is currently open, made of the same dark wood as the base, and is opaque with no transparency. The lid is attached with visible hinges and can be closed to cover the platter.
